9	type heartbeatStore interface {
10		TouchClaimHeartbeat(string) error
11	}
12	
13	// WithHeartbeat runs fn while periodically calling store.TouchClaimHeartbeat
14	// on a background goroutine at the given interval. The goroutine is stopped
15	// and waited for before WithHeartbeat returns.
16	func WithHeartbeat[T any](ctx context.Context, beadID string, interval time.Duration, store heartbeatStore, fn func() (T, error)) (T, error) {
17		ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
18		defer ticker.Stop()
19		return withHeartbeatCh(ctx, beadID, ticker.C, store, fn)
20	}
21	
22	// withHeartbeatCh is the injectable variant used by tests to supply a stub tick channel.
23	func withHeartbeatCh[T any](ctx context.Context, beadID string, tickCh <-chan time.Time, store heartbeatStore, fn func() (T, error)) (T, error) {
24		hbCtx, hbCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
25		var wg sync.WaitGroup
26		wg.Add(1)
27		go func() {
28			defer wg.Done()
29			for {
30				select {
31				case <-hbCtx.Done():
32					return
33				case <-tickCh:
34					_ = store.TouchClaimHeartbeat(beadID)
35				}
36			}
37		}()
38		result, err := fn()
39		hbCancel()
40		wg.Wait()
41		return result, err
42	}

17	// ADR-022 §Worker-server interface (rev 5). Workers are autonomous; the
18	// server's view of who's working is derived from best-effort event reports.
19	// This file implements the three ingestion endpoints:
20	//
21	//   POST /api/workers/register        — issue a worker_id for an identity envelope
22	//   POST /api/workers/{id}/event      — append a worker-emitted event
23	//   POST /api/workers/{id}/backfill   — replay buffered events after reconnect
24	//
25	// In-memory derived view backed by an append-only JSONL log at
26	// .ddx/server/worker-events.jsonl under the server's WorkingDir. Both the
27	// registry and the log are best-effort: if the server crashes the workers
28	// keep operating and re-register on next probe.
29	
30	const workerIngestMaxBodyBytes = 1 << 20 // 1 MiB cap on register/event/backfill payloads.
31	
32	// errUnknownWorker signals that a worker_id is not present in the in-memory
33	// registry. Surfaced as 410 Gone so the worker re-registers within the same
34	// probe cycle (ADR-022 §Probe + freshness state model).
35	var errUnknownWorker = errors.New("unknown_worker")
36	
37	// workerIdentity is the thin envelope POSTed on /api/workers/register.
38	type workerIdentity struct {
39		ProjectRoot  string    `json:"project_root"`
40		Harness      string    `json:"harness"`
41		Model        string    `json:"model,omitempty"`
42		ExecutorPID  int       `json:"executor_pid"`
43		ExecutorHost string    `json:"executor_host"`
44		StartedAt    time.Time `json:"started_at"`
45	}
46	
47	// workerEvent mirrors a single bead-event the worker would write to its
48	// local event log. The wire payload is intentionally minimal — the bead's
49	// local log remains the authoritative copy.
50	type workerEvent struct {
51		BeadID    string          `json:"bead_id"`
52		AttemptID string          `json:"attempt_id"`
53		Kind      string          `json:"kind"`
54		Body      json.RawMessage `json:"body,omitempty"`
55	}
56	
57	// workerBackfillRequest carries the worker's NotConnected ring buffer.
58	// dropped=true means oldest events were silently dropped at the worker
59	// (rev 5: HadDroppedBackfill flag surfaces "consult bead-local logs").
60	type workerBackfillRequest struct {
61		Events  []workerEvent `json:"events"`
62		Dropped bool          `json:"dropped,omitempty"`
63	}
64	
65	// workerRecord is the per-worker derived state the server holds in memory.
66	type workerRecord struct {
67		WorkerID            string         `json:"worker_id"`
68		Identity            workerIdentity `json:"identity"`
69		RegisteredAt        time.Time      `json:"registered_at"`
70		LastEventAt         time.Time      `json:"last_event_at"`
71		MirrorFailuresCount int            `json:"mirror_failures_count"`
72		HadDroppedBackfill  bool           `json:"had_dropped_backfill"`
73		CurrentBead         string         `json:"current_bead,omitempty"`
74		CurrentAttempt      string         `json:"current_attempt,omitempty"`
75	}
76	
77	// workerIngestRegistry holds the in-memory derived view + owns the
78	// append-only event log. All exported methods are safe for concurrent use.
79	type workerIngestRegistry struct {
80		mu       sync.Mutex
81		workers  map[string]*workerRecord
82		logPath  string
83		logFile  *os.File
84		openOnce sync.Once
85		openErr  error
86	}
87	
88	func newWorkerIngestRegistry(workingDir string) *workerIngestRegistry {
89		return &workerIngestRegistry{
90			workers: make(map[string]*workerRecord),
91			logPath: filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "server", "worker-events.jsonl"),
92		}
93	}
94	
95	// register stores a new worker record and returns the issued worker_id.
96	func (r *workerIngestRegistry) register(id workerIdentity) *workerRecord {
97		now := time.Now().UTC()
98		rec := &workerRecord{
99			WorkerID:     newWorkerID(),
100			Identity:     id,
101			RegisteredAt: now,
102			LastEventAt:  now,
103		}
104		r.mu.Lock()
105		r.workers[rec.WorkerID] = rec
106		r.mu.Unlock()
107		return rec
108	}
109	
110	// recordEvent appends ev to the JSONL log and refreshes the worker's
111	// last_event_at timestamp. Returns errUnknownWorker if workerID is not
112	// registered.
113	func (r *workerIngestRegistry) recordEvent(workerID string, ev workerEvent) error {
114		r.mu.Lock()
115		rec, ok := r.workers[workerID]
116		if !ok {
117			r.mu.Unlock()
118			return errUnknownWorker
119		}
120		rec.LastEventAt = time.Now().UTC()
121		if ev.BeadID != "" {
122			rec.CurrentBead = ev.BeadID
123		}
124		if ev.AttemptID != "" {
125			rec.CurrentAttempt = ev.AttemptID
126		}
127		r.mu.Unlock()
128		return r.append(workerID, ev)
129	}
130	
131	// recordBackfill appends every event in the buffer; flips
132	// HadDroppedBackfill when the worker reports dropped overflow.
133	func (r *workerIngestRegistry) recordBackfill(workerID string, req workerBackfillRequest) error {
134		r.mu.Lock()
135		rec, ok := r.workers[workerID]
136		if !ok {
137			r.mu.Unlock()
138			return errUnknownWorker
139		}
140		if req.Dropped {
141			rec.HadDroppedBackfill = true
142		}
143		if len(req.Events) > 0 {
144			rec.LastEventAt = time.Now().UTC()
145			last := req.Events[len(req.Events)-1]
146			if last.BeadID != "" {
147				rec.CurrentBead = last.BeadID
148			}
149			if last.AttemptID != "" {
150				rec.CurrentAttempt = last.AttemptID
151			}
152		}
153		r.mu.Unlock()
154		for _, ev := range req.Events {
155			if err := r.append(workerID, ev); err != nil {
156				return err
157			}
158		}
159		return nil
160	}
161	
162	// snapshot returns a copy of the registry for read-only inspection (tests
163	// and future GraphQL consumers). The returned slice is a stable copy; the
164	// records themselves are pointers into the registry and must be treated
165	// as read-only.
166	func (r *workerIngestRegistry) snapshot() []*workerRecord {
167		r.mu.Lock()
168		defer r.mu.Unlock()
169		out := make([]*workerRecord, 0, len(r.workers))
170		for _, rec := range r.workers {
171			copy := *rec
172			out = append(out, &copy)
173		}
174		return out
175	}
176	
177	// loggedEvent is the on-disk shape of a single line in worker-events.jsonl.
178	// Wraps the worker-supplied event with the receiving worker_id and the
179	// server's timestamp.
180	type loggedEvent struct {
181		WorkerID  string          `json:"worker_id"`
182		Timestamp time.Time       `json:"timestamp"`
183		BeadID    string          `json:"bead_id,omitempty"`
184		AttemptID string          `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
185		Kind      string          `json:"kind"`
186		Body      json.RawMessage `json:"body,omitempty"`
187	}
188	
189	func (r *workerIngestRegistry) append(workerID string, ev workerEvent) error {
190		r.mu.Lock()
191		defer r.mu.Unlock()
192		r.openOnce.Do(func() {
193			if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(r.logPath), 0o755); err != nil {
194				r.openErr = err
195				return
196			}
197			f, err := os.OpenFile(r.logPath, os.O_APPEND|os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
198			if err != nil {
199				r.openErr = err
200				return
201			}
202			r.logFile = f
203		})
204		if r.openErr != nil {
205			return r.openErr
206		}
207		line, err := json.Marshal(loggedEvent{
208			WorkerID:  workerID,
209			Timestamp: time.Now().UTC(),
210			BeadID:    ev.BeadID,
211			AttemptID: ev.AttemptID,
212			Kind:      ev.Kind,
213			Body:      ev.Body,
214		})
215		if err != nil {
216			return err
217		}
218		line = append(line, '\n')
219		_, err = r.logFile.Write(line)
220		return err
221	}
222	
223	func newWorkerID() string {
224		var b [12]byte
225		if _, err := rand.Read(b[:]); err != nil {
226			// crypto/rand failure is unrecoverable; fall back to a timestamp
227			// stub so the path is non-empty (registration still succeeds and
228			// the operator sees a malformed-looking ID in the UI).
229			return fmt.Sprintf("wkr-%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
230		}
231		return "wkr-" + hex.EncodeToString(b[:])
232	}

234	// --- HTTP handlers -----------------------------------------------------
235	
236	func (s *Server) handleWorkerRegister(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
237		var id workerIdentity
238		if err := decodeWorkerIngestBody(r, &id); err != nil {
239			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": err.Error()})
240			return
241		}
242		if id.ProjectRoot == "" {
243			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": "project_root required"})
244			return
245		}
246		rec := s.workerIngest.register(id)
247		writeJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]string{"worker_id": rec.WorkerID})
248	}
249	
250	func (s *Server) handleWorkerEvent(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
251		workerID := r.PathValue("id")
252		if workerID == "" {
253			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": "worker id required"})
254			return
255		}
256		var ev workerEvent
257		if err := decodeWorkerIngestBody(r, &ev); err != nil {
258			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": err.Error()})
259			return
260		}
261		if ev.Kind == "" {
262			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": "kind required"})
263			return
264		}
265		if err := s.workerIngest.recordEvent(workerID, ev); err != nil {
266			if errors.Is(err, errUnknownWorker) {
267				writeJSON(w, http.StatusGone, map[string]string{"error": "unknown_worker"})
268				return
269			}
270			writeJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{"error": err.Error()})
271			return
272		}
273		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)
274	}
275	
276	func (s *Server) handleWorkerBackfill(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
277		workerID := r.PathValue("id")
278		if workerID == "" {
279			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": "worker id required"})
280			return
281		}
282		var req workerBackfillRequest
283		if err := decodeWorkerIngestBody(r, &req); err != nil {
284			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": err.Error()})
285			return
286		}
287		if err := s.workerIngest.recordBackfill(workerID, req); err != nil {
288			if errors.Is(err, errUnknownWorker) {
289				writeJSON(w, http.StatusGone, map[string]string{"error": "unknown_worker"})
290				return
291			}
292			writeJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{"error": err.Error()})
293			return
294		}
295		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)
296	}

298	// workerIngestView is the wire shape returned by GET /api/workers — a
299	// flattened snapshot of the runtime registry used by `legacy agent doctor`
300	// (ADR-022 step 6). The on-disk .ddx/workers/<id>/status.json layout
301	// remains the fallback source for one alpha release.
302	type workerIngestView struct {
303		WorkerID            string    `json:"worker_id"`
304		ProjectRoot         string    `json:"project_root"`
305		Harness             string    `json:"harness,omitempty"`
306		Model               string    `json:"model,omitempty"`
307		RegisteredAt        time.Time `json:"registered_at"`
308		LastEventAt         time.Time `json:"last_event_at"`
309		MirrorFailuresCount int       `json:"mirror_failures_count"`
310		HadDroppedBackfill  bool      `json:"had_dropped_backfill,omitempty"`
311		Freshness           string    `json:"freshness"`
312	}
313	
314	// handleWorkerIngestList serves GET /api/workers: a snapshot of the
315	// in-memory runtime registry (ADR-022 rev 5). Empty list when no workers
316	// have registered. Fields mirror workerRecord plus a derived freshness
317	// classification.
318	func (s *Server) handleWorkerIngestList(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
319		snap := s.workerIngest.snapshot()
320		now := time.Now().UTC()
321		out := make([]workerIngestView, 0, len(snap))
322		for _, rec := range snap {
323			out = append(out, workerIngestView{
324				WorkerID:            rec.WorkerID,
325				ProjectRoot:         rec.Identity.ProjectRoot,
326				Harness:             rec.Identity.Harness,
327				Model:               rec.Identity.Model,
328				RegisteredAt:        rec.RegisteredAt,
329				LastEventAt:         rec.LastEventAt,
330				MirrorFailuresCount: rec.MirrorFailuresCount,
331				HadDroppedBackfill:  rec.HadDroppedBackfill,
332				Freshness:           freshnessState(rec, now),
333			})
334		}
335		sort.Slice(out, func(i, j int) bool { return out[i].RegisteredAt.After(out[j].RegisteredAt) })
336		writeJSON(w, http.StatusOK, out)
337	}
338	
339	func decodeWorkerIngestBody(r *http.Request, v any) error {
340		r.Body = http.MaxBytesReader(nil, r.Body, workerIngestMaxBodyBytes)
341		if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(v); err != nil {
342			return fmt.Errorf("invalid request body: %w", err)
343		}
344		return nil
345	}
346	
347	// freshnessState classifies a worker by recency of its last_event_at against
348	// the probe interval (ADR-022 rev 5: connected ≤2× probe; stale ≤10× probe;
349	// disconnected otherwise). Probe default is 30s, hard-coded here because the
350	// per-worker poll_interval is not part of the rev-5 register payload.
351	func freshnessState(rec *workerRecord, now time.Time) string {
352		const probeInterval = 30 * time.Second
353		age := now.Sub(rec.LastEventAt)
354		switch {
355		case age <= 2*probeInterval:
356			return "connected"
357		case age <= 10*probeInterval:
358			return "stale"
359		default:
360			return "disconnected"
361		}
362	}

26	// TestWorkFocusReportsInterventionQueue verifies that proposed/operator-attention
27	// beads appear in human_required, and dependency-waiting beads appear
28	// in blocked_or_planning.
29	func TestWorkFocusReportsInterventionQueue(t *testing.T) {
30		blocker := &bead.Bead{
31			ID:    "ddx-focus-blocker",
32			Title: "Blocker bead",
33		}
34		// Proposed bead (operator attention required).
35		nhBead := &bead.Bead{
36			ID:     "ddx-focus-nh",
37			Title:  "Needs human attention",
38			Status: bead.StatusProposed,
39			Labels: []string{bead.LabelNeedsHuman},
40			Extra: map[string]any{
41				bead.ExtraNeedsHumanReason: "review returned terminal BLOCK",
42			},
43		}
44		// Dependency-waiting bead (planning required before work can proceed).
45		depBlocked := &bead.Bead{
46			ID:    "ddx-focus-dep",
47			Title: "Dep-blocked planning bead",
48		}
49		depBlocked.AddDep(blocker.ID, "blocks")
50	
51		env := setupWorkFocusEnv(t, blocker, nhBead, depBlocked)
52		root := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir).NewRootCommand()
53	
54		out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "focus", "--json")
55		require.NoError(t, err)
56	
57		var report WorkFocusReport
58		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &report))
59	
60		// Proposed bead must appear in human_required.
61		var foundNH bool
62		for _, item := range report.HumanRequired {
63			if item.ID == nhBead.ID {
64				foundNH = true
65				assert.Equal(t, "review returned terminal BLOCK", item.Reason)
66			}
67		}
68		assert.True(t, foundNH, "proposed bead must appear in human_required")
69	
70		// Proposed bead must NOT appear in blocked_or_planning.
71		for _, item := range report.BlockedOrPlanning {
72			assert.NotEqual(t, nhBead.ID, item.ID, "proposed bead must not appear in blocked_or_planning")
73		}
74	
75		// Dependency-waiting bead must appear in blocked_or_planning.
76		var foundDep bool
77		for _, item := range report.BlockedOrPlanning {
78			if item.ID == depBlocked.ID {
79				foundDep = true
80				assert.Equal(t, bead.BlockerKindDependency, item.BlockerKind)
81			}
82		}
83		assert.True(t, foundDep, "dependency-waiting bead must appear in blocked_or_planning")
84	}

3	import (
4		"bufio"

6		"net/http/httptest"

17	// TestWorker_RealAttemptEvents_FlowToServer is the wired-in end-to-end
18	// proof for ADR-022 step 2: it stands up the production server HTTP path
19	// (httptest.Server wrapping a real serverpkg.Server), points the worker at
20	// it via the production server.addr discovery (XDG_DATA_HOME), spawns a
21	// real `ddx work --once` subprocess against a fixture project, and
22	// asserts that the worker's bead-attempt loop events appear in the
23	// server's derived view (the worker-events.jsonl log) within 5s.
24	//
25	// This exercises the entire wire: writeLoopEvent → loopSink → TeeJSONL →
26	// workerprobe.Probe → POST /api/workers/register + /api/workers/{id}/event
27	// (or /backfill on the immediate first probe before any events fire).
28	func TestWorker_RealAttemptEvents_FlowToServer(t *testing.T) {
29		if testing.Short() {
30			t.Skip("integration test: spawns ddx work subprocess")
31		}
32	
33		// Use a 'standard' fixture (5 sample beads) so the loop has at least
34		// one bead to claim → at least one bead-scoped event flows. We don't
35		// require the bead to succeed; ddx work will emit loop.start +
36		// bead.claimed before any harness invocation.
37		// Build a fresh ddx binary from the current tree so the subprocess
38		// includes the workerprobe wiring under test (cannot rely on $PATH ddx
39		// being current). Build before NewFixtureRepo so the same binary seeds
40		// the fixture, avoiding two builds.
41		binDir := t.TempDir()
42		binPath := filepath.Join(binDir, "ddx")
43		build := exec.Command("go", "build", "-buildvcs=false", "-o", binPath, ".")
44		build.Dir = repoCLIDir(t)
45		if out, err := build.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
46			t.Fatalf("go build ddx: %v\n%s", err, out)
47		}
48		t.Setenv("DDX_BIN", binPath)
49	
50		proj := buildFixtureRepo(t, binPath, "standard")
51		bin := binPath
52	
53		// Real server — same constructor, same Handler, same requireTrusted gate.
54		srv := serverpkg.New(":0", proj)
55		ts := httptest.NewServer(srv.Handler())
56		defer ts.Close()
57	
58		// Production discovery path: write server.addr under XDG_DATA_HOME so
59		// the worker subprocess (running with the same XDG_DATA_HOME) finds it
60		// via serverpkg.ReadServerAddr.
61		xdg := t.TempDir()
62		addrDir := filepath.Join(xdg, "ddx")
63		if err := os.MkdirAll(addrDir, 0o700); err != nil {
64			t.Fatalf("mkdir addr dir: %v", err)
65		}
66		addrPath := filepath.Join(addrDir, "server.addr")
67		addrPayload, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
68			"url": ts.URL,
69			"pid": os.Getpid(), // alive so ReadServerAddr accepts it
70		})
71		if err := os.WriteFile(addrPath, addrPayload, 0o600); err != nil {
72			t.Fatalf("write server.addr: %v", err)
73		}
74	
75		// Spawn `ddx work --once`. We deliberately pass --no-review with the
76		// required acknowledgement flag and a bogus harness so the worker exits
77		// quickly after emitting loop.start + bead.claimed; the harness failure is
78		// irrelevant to this test, which asserts only that events flowed through the
79		// probe.
80		cmd := exec.Command(bin, "work", "--once", "--no-review", "--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing",
81			"--harness", "noop", "--project", proj)
82		home, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-workerprobe-home-*")
83		if err != nil {
84			t.Fatalf("create subprocess home: %v", err)
85		}
86		t.Cleanup(func() {
87			removeTreeWithRetry(t, home)
88		})
89		cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(),
90			"XDG_DATA_HOME="+xdg,
91			"HOME="+home,
92		)
93		cmd.Dir = proj
94		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
95		t.Logf("ddx work exit: err=%v\noutput:\n%s", err, out)
96	
97		// Wait up to 5s for the server's worker-events.jsonl log to receive at
98		// least one event mirrored from the worker subprocess.
99		logPath := filepath.Join(proj, ".ddx", "server", "worker-events.jsonl")
100		deadline := time.Now().Add(5 * time.Second)
101		var lines []string
102		for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
103			lines = readLogLines(t, logPath)
104			if len(lines) > 0 {
105				break
106			}
107			time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)
108		}
109		if len(lines) == 0 {
110			t.Fatalf("no worker events appeared in %s within 5s; subprocess output:\n%s", logPath, out)
111		}
112	
113		// Sanity-check: at least one mirrored event should be a loop.* or
114		// bead.* envelope identifiable by the writeLoopEvent shape (kind field
115		// in the loggedEvent wrapper).
116		var kinds []string
117		for _, line := range lines {
118			var le struct {
119				Kind string `json:"kind"`
120			}
121			_ = json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &le)
122			kinds = append(kinds, le.Kind)
123		}
124		t.Logf("mirrored event kinds: %v", kinds)
125		hasExpected := false
126		for _, k := range kinds {
127			if strings.HasPrefix(k, "loop.") || strings.HasPrefix(k, "bead.") {
128				hasExpected = true
129				break
130			}
131		}
132		if !hasExpected {
133			t.Errorf("expected at least one loop.* or bead.* event mirrored, got kinds=%v", kinds)
134		}
135	}

137	func removeTreeWithRetry(t *testing.T, path string) {
138		t.Helper()
139		var err error
140		for i := 0; i < 30; i++ {
141			err = os.RemoveAll(path)
142			if err == nil || os.IsNotExist(err) {
143				return
144			}
145			time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
146		}
147		t.Fatalf("remove temp tree %s: %v", path, err)
148	}

150	// repoCLIDir walks up from this file to find the cli/ directory (which
151	// holds go.mod). Used to build a fresh ddx binary for the integration test.
152	func repoCLIDir(t *testing.T) string {
153		t.Helper()
154		wd, err := os.Getwd()
155		if err != nil {
156			t.Fatalf("getwd: %v", err)
157		}
158		dir := wd
159		for {
160			if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(dir, "go.mod")); err == nil {
161				return dir
162			}
163			parent := filepath.Dir(dir)
164			if parent == dir {
165				t.Fatalf("could not locate go.mod from %s", wd)
166			}
167			dir = parent
168		}
169	}

171	// buildFixtureRepo runs scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh with the supplied ddx
172	// binary and profile, returning the created project root.
173	func buildFixtureRepo(t *testing.T, bin, profile string) string {
174		t.Helper()
175		root := filepath.Dir(repoCLIDir(t))
176		script := filepath.Join(root, "scripts", "build-fixture-repo.sh")
177		dest := t.TempDir()
178	
179		cmd := exec.Command("bash", script, dest, "--profile", profile)
180		cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), "DDX_BIN="+bin)
181		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
182		if err != nil {
183			t.Fatalf("build-fixture-repo.sh failed (profile=%s): %v\n%s", profile, err, out)
184		}
185		return dest
186	}

188	// readLogLines returns each newline-separated line of the file at path,
189	// or nil if the file does not yet exist.
190	func readLogLines(t *testing.T, path string) []string {
191		t.Helper()
192		f, err := os.Open(path)
193		if err != nil {
194			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
195				return nil
196			}
197			t.Fatalf("open %s: %v", path, err)
198		}
199		defer f.Close()
200		var out []string
201		sc := bufio.NewScanner(f)
202		for sc.Scan() {
203			out = append(out, sc.Text())
204		}
205		return out
206	}

113	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
114		mode := r.Mode
115		if mode == "" {
116			if r.Once {
117				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
118			} else {
119				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
120			}
121		}
122		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
123		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
124			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
125		}
126		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
127			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
128		}
129		return mode, idleInterval
130	}
131	
132	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
133	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
134	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
135	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
136	
137	// ReviewerSkippedEmptyDiffEventKind is the event kind emitted when the
138	// reviewer dispatch is skipped because the implementer produced no commits
139	// (BaseRev == ResultRev). Per ADR-024 P1: cheapest first — paying a reviewer
140	// to evaluate an empty diff is unjustified cost.
141	const ReviewerSkippedEmptyDiffEventKind = "reviewer-skipped-empty-diff"
142	
143	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
144	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
145	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
146	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
147	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
148	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
149	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
150	
151	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
152	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
153	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
154	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
155	// advance the base branch.
156	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
157	
158	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
159	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
160	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
161	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
162	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
163	// worker.
164	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
165	
166	// ProviderUnavailableCooldown is the retry pause for route/provider outages
167	// where no configured powerClass can currently run the bead. This is transient
168	// scheduler state, not bead quality evidence, so it must not become a durable
169	// triage:needs-investigation park.
170	const ProviderUnavailableCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
171	
172	// SmartRetryCooldown is the queue-fairness pause after a real no_changes
173	// implementation attempt asks for status=open/smart retry. The bead remains
174	// open with its powerClass hint, but a watch worker moves on instead of repeating the
175	// same empty-diff attempt every idle cycle.
176	const SmartRetryCooldown = 5 * time.Minute

180	// (baseRev, resultRev) before forwarding to submit. On Blocked, it preserves
181	// the worker's result under refs/ddx/iterations/, records checks-blocked
182	// events on the bead, and returns the synthesised LandResult{Status:"preserved"}

184	// recorded as checks-bypass events and submit runs normally.

16	// fakeServer is a minimal stand-in for the worker-ingest endpoints. Tests
17	// drive Reachable to flip the server's "presence" without tearing down the
18	// httptest.Server.
19	type fakeServer struct {
20		mu        sync.Mutex
21		reachable bool
22		workerID  string
23	
24		registered       int32
25		events           int32
26		backfills        int32
27		lastEvents       []Event
28		lastDropped      bool
29		failNextRegister bool
30	}
31	
32	func newFakeServer(workerID string) *fakeServer {
33		return &fakeServer{reachable: true, workerID: workerID}
34	}
35	
36	func (f *fakeServer) Handler() http.Handler {
37		mux := http.NewServeMux()
38		mux.HandleFunc("/api/workers/register", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
39			f.mu.Lock()
40			if !f.reachable {
41				f.mu.Unlock()
42				http.Error(w, "down", http.StatusServiceUnavailable)
43				return
44			}
45			if f.failNextRegister {
46				f.failNextRegister = false
47				f.mu.Unlock()
48				http.Error(w, "fail", http.StatusInternalServerError)
49				return
50			}
51			atomic.AddInt32(&f.registered, 1)
52			id := f.workerID
53			f.mu.Unlock()
54			_ = json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]string{"worker_id": id})
55		})
56		mux.HandleFunc("/api/workers/", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
57			f.mu.Lock()
58			reachable := f.reachable
59			f.mu.Unlock()
60			if !reachable {
61				http.Error(w, "down", http.StatusServiceUnavailable)
62				return
63			}
64			// Expect /api/workers/{id}/event or /backfill
65			switch {
66			case hasSuffix(r.URL.Path, "/event"):
67				var ev Event
68				if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&ev); err != nil {
69					http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusBadRequest)
70					return
71				}
72				f.mu.Lock()
73				f.lastEvents = append(f.lastEvents, ev)
74				f.mu.Unlock()
75				atomic.AddInt32(&f.events, 1)
76				w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)
77			case hasSuffix(r.URL.Path, "/backfill"):
78				var req struct {
79					Events  []Event `json:"events"`
80					Dropped bool    `json:"dropped"`
81				}
82				if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {
83					http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusBadRequest)
84					return
85				}
86				f.mu.Lock()
87				f.lastEvents = append(f.lastEvents, req.Events...)
88				f.lastDropped = f.lastDropped || req.Dropped
89				f.mu.Unlock()
90				atomic.AddInt32(&f.backfills, 1)
91				w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)
92			default:
93				http.NotFound(w, r)
94			}
95		})
96		return mux
97	}

100		return len(s) >= len(suf) && s[len(s)-len(suf):] == suf

data.text:
Bead ddx-a3589116 implemented in commit 4f0c2ef43. Summary of changes:

- **`internal/workerstatus/liveness.go`** (new): `LivenessRecord` struct + `WriteLiveness`/`ReadLiveness`/`ListLiveness` helpers backing `.ddx/workers/<id>/status.json` with atomic tmp-then-rename.
- **`internal/agent/work/liveness.go`** (new): `SidecarLivenessReporter` writes the sidecar AND emits a `worker.heartbeat` envelope on each tick — fail-open against sink errors.
- **`internal/agent/work/heartbeat.go`**: `WithHeartbeat` now accepts an optional `LivenessReporter`; on each tick it runs `TouchClaimHeartbeat` + `reporter.OnTick`.
- **`internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`**: Builds the reporter when `ProjectRoot+SessionID` is set; calls `SetAttempt` + immediate tick before each `WithHeartbeat` and `ClearAttempt` after.
- **`cmd/work_focus.go`**: New `ActiveWorkers` section reads sidecars within `LivenessTTL` and suppresses the in_progress liveness "unknown" hazard when at least one fresh sidecar is present.
- 5 new tests (`TestWorkerHeartbeat_…`, `TestWorkerLiveness_…` ×2, `TestWorkerProbe_PeriodicHeartbeat…`, `TestWorkFocusReportsActiveLongRunningWorkerFromWorkerStatus`) all pass under the AC6 regex.

Pre-commit hooks green; the three failing tests in the output (`TestReadinessUnavailableOutputIsActionable`, `TestLifecycleHooks_UnpinnedWorkersStillUseProfileSelection`, `TestTry_HooksWired`) were verified as pre-existing failures on the base revision and are unrelated to this bead.
